headline: Halal Industry Revenue Misses RM56B Target by 39%
context: HDC reported Malaysia's halal export revenue at RM34.2 billion in 2025, falling 39% short of the RM56 billion target set under the Halal Industry Master Plan 2030, with competition from Turkey, UAE, and Indonesia intensifying.

cards.big: RM56 billion halal export target missed by 39% — Malaysia's first-mover advantage eroded by 14 competing nations
cards.sub: Indonesia's halal exports grew 28% in 2025 while Malaysia's grew only 6% — market share slipping fast

cards.big: Global halal market worth RM8.4 trillion but Malaysia captures only 0.4% despite pioneering halal certification
cards.sub: Halal certification costs RM15,000-RM45,000 per product — prohibitive for SMEs that form 87% of halal producers

cards.big: 72% of Malaysian halal exports are ingredients and commodities — not branded consumer products with higher margins
cards.sub: Country produces palm oil and rubber for others' halal brands — value capture remains at the bottom of the chain

cards.big: JAKIM halal certification process takes average 8 months — compared to 6 weeks for UAE's ESMA and 3 months for Indonesia's MUI
cards.sub: HDC, JAKIM, MITI, and MATRADE share halal industry responsibility with no single lead agency or unified strategy

cards.big: Malaysia invented modern halal certification — then let 14 countries copy, improve, and overtake the original
Should JAKIM halal certification be fast-tracked to 60 days with digital processing to match regional competitors?
